4. `planner.llm_skipped_rate` per org visible in system-pnl; if it's degrading for a specific org, investigate before broader rollout.

No automatic rollout — each org is a conscious decision based on traffic and data shape.

---

## 22. Open questions for implementation

Tracked as `// TODO(spec-open-N)` in code; surfaced at PR review time.

1. **`canonicalDataService` extensions — drizzle query shape.** Each new handler (e.g. `listInactiveContacts`) needs the exact drizzle query. Handler body shown in §12.3 is directional; the implementer confirms the current schema's column names and indexes during P1. If any column the handler needs is missing, that's an open question, not a blocker — the spec falls through to live for that query class until canonical catches up.
2. **~~Dependency-cruiser rule for read-only enforcement.~~ Closed.** v1 ships `scripts/verify-crm-query-planner-read-only.sh` as a repo-style static gate (see §13.3 / §16.6). Matches the existing `verify-*.sh` convention; no dependency-cruiser adoption required.
3. **Stage 2 cache persistence.** v1 is in-process; process restart loses the cache. Open question: should we persist to Redis for multi-instance deployments? Decision point: once we observe `planner.llm_skipped_rate` drift across instances, not before. The hit-rate metric is the trigger.
4. **Schema-context compression — concrete algorithm.** §11.11 specifies "entity-level summaries + top-N fields per entity + per-query filtering." The exact summarisation algorithm (top-N by what? frequency? alphabetical?) is deferred to implementation — initial cut is static-ranked (most commonly-used fields per entity, hardcoded list); pressure-test results inform whether to replace with frequency-weighted.
5. **LLM intent-parse timeout.** How long before Stage 3 is abandoned? No explicit v1 timeout specified; `llmRouter` has its own default. Open question: does the planner need a stricter override? Recommend no — router defaults are tuned for the cost envelope and we don't want to introduce a second timeout source.
6. **Per-run cost ledger attribution for planner calls.** The planner's Stage 3 call is a normal `llmRouter` call and lands in the ledger automatically. Open question: do we need a `featureTag: 'crm-query-planner'` dimension in the per-run cost panel so users can see "of this run's cost, X cents came from CRM queries"? Recommend yes — small extension, high ops value.

---

## Deferred Items

Consolidated list of every v1 exclusion with an explicit verdict. Authoring-checklist §7 requires a single `## Deferred Items` section; the items below are the canonical source. References in §1.2, §1.3, §2.1, §9.2, §15.4, §17, §22 point here.

Verdict legend:
- **DEFER-V2** — known follow-on scope; will be picked up in a dedicated follow-on when the trigger fires
- **WON'T-DO** — explicit non-goal, not planned to ship regardless of signal
- **BUILD-WHEN-SIGNAL** — ship only when a named metric or real incident justifies it

| Item | Verdict | Trigger / Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Hybrid patterns beyond `canonical_base_with_live_filter` (§1.2) | BUILD-WHEN-SIGNAL | `planner.hybrid_unsupported_rate` climbs AND pressure-test (§20.3) surfaces a second pattern worth codifying |
| Semantic / fuzzy plan-cache match (§1.2) | BUILD-WHEN-SIGNAL | Exact-match `planner.llm_skipped_rate` plateaus below target before alias coverage exhausts — i.e. deterministic coverage is blocked by phrasing drift, not missing aliases |
| Query Memory Layer surfacing (§1.2, §17.3) | DEFER-V2 | Owned by a follow-on spec — Layer's promotion dashboard + workflow. Event shape is v1-locked so v2 can consume without migration |
| Non-GHL live executors (HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive) (§1.2) | DEFER-V2 | Same planner, new adapter — ships when a second CRM integration is in-scope; no v1 work |
| Agent-facing `crm.schema_describe` skill (§1.2, §2.1 brief §11.3) | WON'T-DO (unless signal) | Automatic Stage 3 injection makes a round-trip skill redundant; revisit only if schema injection cost exceeds the per-query ceiling |
| Brief-surface export / saved-segment affordances (§1.2) | DEFER-V2 | Owned by the Brief-surface branch |
| External MCP exposure of the planner (§1.2) | WON'T-DO | Architectural non-goal — planner is an internal primitive; external agents hit the Brief surface |
| Universal Brief chat surface (§1.2) | DEFER-V2 | Owned by a separate branch |
| Free-text writes via the planner (§1.2) | WON'T-DO | Writes remain review-gated `crm.*` skills; planner is read-only by design |
| Plan-cache persistence (Redis / multi-instance) (§22.3) | BUILD-WHEN-SIGNAL | `planner.llm_skipped_rate` drift observed across instances (deployment mode dependent) |
| Schema-change-driven cache invalidation (§9.2) | DEFER-V2 | 60s TTL is sufficient in v1; revisit if TTL-drift causes real staleness incidents |
| Batch-email approval card (one approval, N sends) (§15.4) | DEFER-V2 | Requires a new batch `crm.send_email_batch` action slug in `actionRegistry`; v1 ships single-contact only |
| `topAliasSimilarity` analytics on `planner.stage1_missed` (§17.1) | BUILD-WHEN-SIGNAL | Surfacing near-miss aliases is useful only once Stage 1 coverage plateaus below target |
| Dependency-cruiser rule for read-only enforcement (§22.2) | WON'T-DO (closed) | Replaced by `scripts/verify-crm-query-planner-read-only.sh` static gate; no dependency-cruiser adoption required |
| Stopgap "Ask CRM" standalone panel (§3 context) | WON'T-DO | Brief surface is the CRM question UX; no second entry point |
| Per-subaccount rate-limit budget (§2.1 brief §11.5) | BUILD-WHEN-SIGNAL | Real traffic surfaces cross-subaccount starvation on `getProviderRateLimiter('ghl')` |

Each entry has a verdict and a trigger or reason. Items with `BUILD-WHEN-SIGNAL` include the specific metric or condition that would unblock them — absence of that signal is the reason not to ship now.

---

## 23. Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Stage 3 LLM produces plans the validator keeps rejecting, yielding high `ambiguous_intent` rates | M | M | Pressure test (§20.3) exposes this before P2 ships; add few-shot examples to prompt if needed |
| Canonical handler extensions don't match existing drizzle schema shape | M | L | P1 catches this at implementation; blocker is isolated to that one handler |
| Hybrid executor's 10-call cap fires on legitimate queries, frustrating users | L | M | `hybrid_unsupported_rate` metric catches this; raise cap if real queries are consistently below cap but still failing |
| LLM cost exceeds budget before `planner.llm_skipped_rate` improves | M | M | Per-query cent ceiling (§16.2) is the circuit breaker; `runCostBreaker` is the backstop |
| Real operator phrasing doesn't match conservative v1 alias list, Stage 1 hit rate is low | H | M | Pressure test + alias-list tuning; synonym map is the main lever. First 2 weeks of real traffic will reveal the biggest misses |
| Planner-vs-Orchestrator boundary drift (implementer teaches Orchestrator to pre-classify) | L | H | Brief §1 + this spec §3 explicitly state the boundary. `pr-reviewer` catches violations. KNOWLEDGE.md entry pins the rule |
| Schema-context injection bloats context window, Stage 3 latency climbs | M | L | Token budget (§16.1) caps injection; pressure test measures actual sizes |
| Query Memory Layer assumptions in event schema turn out wrong when we actually build it | L | L | v2's problem; v1 captures all the obvious fields, v2 can extend |

---

## 24. Success criteria

A senior reviewer can answer yes to all of:

1. **The planner is the single entry point for CRM reads.** No surface — chat, agent tool call, direct API — reads CRM data without going through the planner.
2. **Deterministic-first is measurable.** `planner.llm_skipped_rate` reports above 0 on day one of P3, and rises over time as aliases and cache accumulate.
3. **All 8 registry entries resolve via Stage 1 for their primary phrasings.** Every alias in the registry hits the registry-backed matcher.
4. **Stage 3 LLM output always passes or cleanly fails.** No in-between states reach the executor. Validator authority is observable via `planner.validation_failed` vs `planner.classified` event counts.
5. **Read-only is statically enforced.** No write helper is in scope for the live executor. Attempted write-helper imports fail `scripts/verify-crm-query-planner-read-only.sh` at CI, before any runtime code executes.
6. **Cost is bounded per query and per run.** Per-query cent ceiling (§16.2) is checked post-Stage-3 — after parse + any escalation — and fires before executor dispatch; per-run ceiling is the backstop via `runCostBreaker` and is checked both before Stage 3 and before live-executor dispatch.
7. **Failure is a first-class state.** Every error result carries `suggestions[]` that are valid follow-up Briefs. No dead ends.
8. **Rate-limit fair-queueing holds.** ClientPulse polling and planner live queries both use `getProviderRateLimiter('ghl')`; neither starves the other.
9. **Observability feeds the future learning loop.** Event payloads include `stageResolved`, `canonicalCandidateKey`, `intentHash`, `hitCount` — the data a Query Memory Layer needs to identify promotion candidates.
10. **Pressure test run and documented.** Results written to `tasks/builds/crm-query-planner/pressure-test-results.md` before P2 ships; any alias / synonym / prompt tunings committed alongside the code.
11. **No schema changes.** `db/schema/` unchanged; migration count unchanged.
12. **No new governance primitives.** `llmRouter`, `runCostBreaker`, `getProviderRateLimiter`, `withPrincipalContext` all reused as-is.

If yes to all twelve, P3 is ready for `pr-reviewer` and PR to main.

---

 succeeded in 306ms:
1:// Shared wire types for the Brief result contract.
2:// Spec: docs/brief-result-contract.md
3://
4:// These types are the canonical contract between:
5://  - Brief chat surfaces (client) — render artefacts
6://  - Orchestrator / capability skills (server) — produce artefacts
7://  - Downstream capabilities (e.g. CRM Query Planner, on a separate branch)
8://    — emit results in this shape
9://
10:// Any capability that wants to render into a Brief chat must emit one of
11:// these artefact kinds. The shape is designed to support rich rendering
12:// (tables, approval cards, error surfaces with refinement suggestions)
13:// without forcing callers into a specific UI framework.
14://
15:// Versioning: this is v1. Breaking changes require bumping
16:// CONTRACT_VERSION and surfacing a migration path for consumers.
17:// Additive changes (new optional fields, new enum values) do not require
18:// a bump — consumers ignore unknown fields.
19:
20:export const BRIEF_RESULT_CONTRACT_VERSION = 1 as const;
21:
22:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
23:// Shared primitives
24:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25:
26:/**
27: * The canonical entity a structured result describes. 'other' is a pragmatic
28: * escape hatch for non-CRM surfaces (e.g. PR activity, run logs, connector
29: * health). Consumers should treat 'other' as "render rows without entity-
30: * specific affordances like open-record links."
31: */
32:export type BriefResultEntityType =
33:  | 'contacts'
34:  | 'opportunities'
35:  | 'appointments'
36:  | 'conversations'
37:  | 'revenue'
38:  | 'tasks'
39:  | 'runs'
40:  | 'other';
41:
42:/**
43: * Where the data came from. 'canonical' = our ingested tables. 'live' = direct
44: * provider API call. 'hybrid' = combined. Lets the UI flag freshness and the
45: * caller decide whether to cache.
46: */
47:export type BriefResultSource = 'canonical' | 'live' | 'hybrid';
48:
49:/**
50: * Why the result set was truncated. Distinguishing these matters for
51: * suggestion generation — 'result_limit' suggests narrowing filters;
52: * 'cost_limit' suggests a cheaper query; 'time_limit' suggests a longer
53: * window.
54: */
55:export type BriefTruncationReason = 'result_limit' | 'cost_limit' | 'time_limit';
56:
57:/**
58: * A filter the system interpreted from the user's request and applied
59: * to the underlying query. Rendered as chips in the UI so users can see
60: * (and correct) how their intent was parsed.
61: */
62:export interface BriefResultFilter {
63:  /** Canonical field name, e.g. 'tags', 'lastActivityAt', 'stage'. */
64:  field: string;
65:  /** Operator: 'eq' | 'neq' | 'gt' | 'gte' | 'lt' | 'lte' | 'in' | 'contains' | 'between' | 'exists' */
66:  operator: string;
67:  /** Raw value — shape depends on operator (scalar for eq, array for in, tuple for between). */
68:  value: unknown;
69:  /** UI-friendly label, e.g. "Tag is VIP" or "Last activity older than 30 days". */
70:  humanLabel: string;
71:}
72:
73:/**
74: * A follow-up affordance rendered below the result. `intent` must be phrased
75: * so the Orchestrator can parse it as a new Brief (i.e. it's a self-contained
76: * instruction, not a fragment).
77: */
78:export interface BriefResultSuggestion {
79:  /** Short UI label, e.g. "Narrow to last 7 days". */
80:  label: string;
81:  /** Full re-parseable instruction, e.g. "Show VIP contacts inactive 30d in the last 7 days." */
82:  intent: string;
83:  /** Classification for UI grouping. */
84:  kind: 'narrow' | 'broaden' | 'sort' | 'action' | 'other';
85:}
86:
87:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
88:// Structured result — the common "here's what you asked for" artefact
89:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
90:
91:export interface BriefStructuredResult {
92:  kind: 'structured';
93:  /** One-sentence human summary, e.g. "18 VIP contacts inactive 30d". */
94:  summary: string;
95:  entityType: BriefResultEntityType;
96:  filtersApplied: BriefResultFilter[];
97:  /** Rows rendered in the UI table. Schema is entity-dependent — consumers render by entityType. */
98:  rows: Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
99:  /** Total matching count — may exceed rows.length when truncated. */
100:  rowCount: number;
101:  truncated: boolean;
102:  truncationReason?: BriefTruncationReason;
103:  /** Refinement / follow-up suggestions. May be empty. */
104:  suggestions: BriefResultSuggestion[];
105:  /** Actual spend for this result, in cents. Always present. */
106:  costCents: number;
107:  source: BriefResultSource;
108:}
109:
110:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111:// Approval card — for write actions the Orchestrator wants to take
112:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113:
114:export type BriefApprovalRiskLevel = 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
115:
116:export interface BriefApprovalCard {
117:  kind: 'approval';
118:  /** Human summary of the proposed action, e.g. "Send follow-up email to 14 contacts". */
119:  summary: string;
120:  /** Must be a registered actionSlug in server/config/actionRegistry.ts. */
121:  actionSlug: string;
122:  /** Arguments matching the registered action's typed schema. Validated on dispatch. */
123:  actionArgs: Record<string, unknown>;
124:  /** Record IDs the action affects — used for preview rendering and audit. */
125:  affectedRecordIds: string[];
126:  /** Predicted spend for executing the action. Optional because some actions are free. */
127:  estimatedCostCents?: number;
128:  /**
129:   * Risk tier. 'high' forces explicit approval even if the user has auto-approve
130:   * enabled for routine actions. Derived from the action's defaultGateLevel and
131:   * the scope/blast-radius of the args.
132:   */
133:  riskLevel: BriefApprovalRiskLevel;
134:}
135:
136:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
137:// Error result — with refinement suggestions where possible
138:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
139:
140:export type BriefErrorCode =
141:  | 'unsupported_query'
142:  | 'ambiguous_intent'
143:  | 'missing_permission'
144:  | 'cost_exceeded'
145:  | 'rate_limited'
146:  | 'provider_error'
147:  | 'internal_error';
148:
149:export interface BriefErrorResult {
150:  kind: 'error';
151:  errorCode: BriefErrorCode;
152:  /** Plain-English explanation suitable for rendering in chat. */
153:  message: string;
154:  /** Optional refinement suggestions — what the user could try instead. */
155:  suggestions?: BriefResultSuggestion[];
156:}
157:
158:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
159:// Cost preview — returned before execution for user-confirmed dispatch
160:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
161:
162:export interface BriefCostPreview {
163:  predictedCostCents: number;
164:  confidence: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
165:  /** How the prediction was derived — affects how much to trust it. */
166:  basedOn: 'planner_estimate' | 'cached_similar_query' | 'static_heuristic';
167:}

170:// Discriminated union — the top-level artefact type
171:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
172:
173:/**
174: * A single "thing" rendered into a Brief chat by the system. Consumers
175: * discriminate by `kind`. Each new chat turn may produce 0..N artefacts.
176: */
177:export type BriefChatArtefact =
178:  | BriefStructuredResult
179:  | BriefApprovalCard
180:  | BriefErrorResult;
181:

183:// Type guards — convenience for consumers
184:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
185:
186:export function isBriefStructuredResult(a: BriefChatArtefact): a is BriefStructuredResult {
187:  return a.kind === 'structured';
188:}
189:
190:export function isBriefApprovalCard(a: BriefChatArtefact): a is BriefApprovalCard {
191:  return a.kind === 'approval';
192:}

195:  return a.kind === 'error';

1:import { z } from 'zod';
2:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
3:// Action Type Registry — central definition of all action types
4:// Phase 1: TypeScript config object. Phase 2: promotes to DB table.
5:// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
6:
7:export interface RetryPolicy {
8:  maxRetries: number;
9:  strategy: 'exponential_backoff' | 'fixed' | 'none';
10:  retryOn: string[];
11:  doNotRetryOn: string[];
12:}
13:
14:/** MCP ToolAnnotations — maps to the MCP specification's ToolAnnotations type */
15:export interface McpAnnotations {
16:  readOnlyHint: boolean;    // true = does not modify external state
17:  destructiveHint: boolean; // true = may be irreversible
18:  idempotentHint: boolean;  // true = same args = same effect
19:  openWorldHint: boolean;   // true = reaches external systems
20:}
21:
22:/** JSON Schema describing the tool's input parameters */
23:export interface ParameterSchema {
24:  type: 'object';
25:  properties: Record<string, {
26:    type: string;
27:    description?: string;
28:    enum?: string[];
29:    properties?: Record<string, unknown>;
30:    items?: Record<string, unknown>;
31:  }>;
32:  required: string[];
33:  additionalProperties?: boolean;
34:}
35:
36:/**
37: * Execution-model contract for an action handler — declares how the handler
38: * stays safe under retry given the at-least-once execution guarantee. See
39: * docs/improvements-roadmap-spec.md → "Execution model — at-least-once,
40: * idempotent handlers" for the full rationale.
41: *
42: *   - 'read_only'   — no side effects; safe to re-run without any coordination.
43: *   - 'keyed_write' — writes are deduplicated by the caller-supplied
44: *                     idempotencyKey at the DB or external provider layer
45: *                     (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, Idempotency-Key
46: *                     header, etc.).
47: *   - 'locked'      — handler takes a pg advisory lock keyed on the
48: *                     idempotencyKey before the call and releases on exit.
49: *                     Used for irreversible side effects to third parties
50: *                     that have no native dedupe story.
51: */
52:export type IdempotencyStrategy = 'read_only' | 'keyed_write' | 'locked';
53:
54:export interface ActionDefinition {
55:  actionType: string;
56:  description: string;
57:  actionCategory: 'api' | 'worker' | 'browser' | 'devops' | 'mcp';
58:  isExternal: boolean;
59:  defaultGateLevel: 'auto' | 'review' | 'block';
60:  createsBoardTask: boolean;
61:  /** @deprecated Use parameterSchema instead. Kept for backward compat. */
62:  payloadFields: string[];
63:  parameterSchema: z.ZodObject<z.ZodRawShape>;
64:  retryPolicy: RetryPolicy;
65:  mcp?: { annotations: McpAnnotations };
66:
67:  /**
68:   * P0.2 Slice B — required on every entry from Sprint 1 landing onward.
69:   * Enforced by verify-idempotency-strategy-declared.sh.
70:   */
71:  idempotencyStrategy: IdempotencyStrategy;
72:
73:  /**
74:   * P1.1 Layer 3 — declarative scope metadata consumed by the before-tool
75:   * authorisation hook. See P1.1 Layer 3 validateScope() for the check
76:   * implementation. Optional — only actions that operate on tenant-scoped
77:   * resources need to declare scope requirements.
78:   */
79:  scopeRequirements?: {
80:    /** Names of arg fields that must be subaccount IDs the current tenant owns. */
81:    validateSubaccountFields?: string[];
82:    /** Names of arg fields that must be GHL location IDs the current tenant owns. */
83:    validateGhlLocationFields?: string[];
84:    /** If true, run requires `userId` in execution context (no system runs). */
85:    requiresUserContext?: boolean;
86:  };
87:
88:  /** P4.1 — topic tags for intent-based filtering. */
89:  topics?: string[];
90:
91:  /** P4.4 — opt-in to the semantic critique gate when run via economy tier. */
92:  requiresCritiqueGate?: boolean;
93:
94:  /**
95:   * P0.2 Slice C — extended retry behaviour. Overrides retryPolicy's
96:   * default fail-the-run semantics:
97:   *   - 'retry'    — use withBackoff per retryPolicy (default, matches
98:   *                  existing behaviour).
99:   *   - 'skip'     — log the failure, return
100:   *                  { success: false, skipped: true, reason } to the LLM,
101:   *                  and let the agent loop continue.
102:   *   - 'fail_run' — terminate the entire agent run via failure() from
103:   *                  shared/iee/failure.ts.
104:   *   - 'fallback' — return fallbackValue as the result instead of failing.
105:   */
106:  onFailure?: 'retry' | 'skip' | 'fail_run' | 'fallback';
107:  fallbackValue?: unknown;
108:
109:  /**
110:   * P2A — data read-path classification. Every action declares where it
111:   * reads data from so that the canonical-data migration can be tracked:
112:   *   - 'canonical'  — reads normalised data via canonicalDataService.
113:   *   - 'liveFetch'  — hits a provider API directly (not yet migrated).
114:   *   - 'none'       — does not read external data (pure tool / creation).
115:   * Enforced by verify-skill-read-paths.sh — every entry must have this.
116:   */
117:  readPath: 'canonical' | 'liveFetch' | 'none';
118:
119:  /**
120:   * Required when readPath is 'liveFetch'. Documents why this action still
121:   * hits the provider API instead of reading from canonical tables.
122:   */
123:  liveFetchRationale?: string;
124:
125:  /**
126:   * P1.1 Layer 3 — flag to mark methodology skills (pure prompt scaffolds,
127:   * no side effects). When true, the preTool middleware bypasses
128:   * actionService.proposeAction and writes a single audit row with
129:   * reason='methodology_skill'. Distinct from read-only skills because
130:   * methodology skills do not even read from external systems.
131:   */
132:  isMethodology?: boolean;
133:
134:  /**
135:   * P4.1 — universal skills are always merged into every agent's effective
136:   * allowlist and always preserved through the topic filter. See the
137:   * universal-skill contract in docs/improvements-roadmap-spec.md P4.1.
138:   */
139:  isUniversal?: boolean;
140:}
141:

2769:
2770:/**
2771: * Legacy action-slug aliases.
2772: *
2773: * Per contract (l) in tasks/builds/clientpulse/session-1-foundation-spec.md
2774: * §1.3: all inbound action-slug surfaces MUST normalise via `resolveActionSlug`.
2775: * Routes, webhook handlers, queue consumers, anything that receives an
2776: * `action_type` from an external caller.
2777: *
2778: * The migration in `0181_rename_operator_alert.sql` rewrites historical rows,
2779: * but in-flight job payloads, cached definitions, dashboard filters, and
2780: * external callers may still carry the pre-Session-1 slug. Resolving via this
2781: * map is defence in depth — once per process per alias hit is logged so drift
2782: * is visible without swamping the log.
2783: *
2784: * Append-only: entries can be added but never silently removed. Removal
2785: * requires a deliberate code change.
2786: */
2787:export const ACTION_SLUG_ALIASES: Readonly<Record<string, string>> = Object.freeze({
2788:  'clientpulse.operator_alert': 'notify_operator',
2789:  config_update_hierarchy_template: 'config_update_organisation_config',
2790:});
2791:
2792:const loggedAliasHits = new Set<string>();
2793:
2794:/**
2795: * Normalise an inbound action-type slug to its canonical registered form.
2796: *
2797: * Returns the canonical slug if the input matches an alias; returns the input
2798: * unchanged otherwise. First hit per alias per process logs a warning so alias
2799: * consumption is observable without log spam.
2800: */
2801:export function resolveActionSlug(slug: string): string {
2802:  const canonical = ACTION_SLUG_ALIASES[slug];
2803:  if (canonical === undefined) return slug;
2804:  if (!loggedAliasHits.has(slug)) {
2805:    loggedAliasHits.add(slug);
2806:    // eslint-disable-next-line no-console
2807:    console.warn(
2808:      `[action-registry] legacy slug consumed: '${slug}' → '${canonical}'. Update the caller.`,
2809:    );
2810:  }
2811:  return canonical;
2812:}
2813:
2814:/**
2815: * Test-only — reset the log-once set so tests can assert the first-hit log
2816: * fires exactly once per alias per process. Never call from production code.
2817: */
2818:export function __resetActionSlugAliasLogOnceForTests(): void {
2819:  loggedAliasHits.clear();
2820:}
2821:
2822:/** Check if an action type is known. Routes through the alias resolver. */
2823:export function getActionDefinition(actionType: string): ActionDefinition | undefined {
2824:  return ACTION_REGISTRY[resolveActionSlug(actionType)];
2825:}
2826:
2827:/**
2828: * Sprint 5 P4.1 — returns the action types of all universal skills.
2829: * Re-exports from the dependency-free universalSkills.ts so callers
2830: * that already import from actionRegistry don't need to change.
2831: */
2832:import { UNIVERSAL_SKILL_NAMES } from './universalSkills.js';
2833:export { UNIVERSAL_SKILL_NAMES };
2834:export function getUniversalSkillNames(): string[] {
2835:  return [...UNIVERSAL_SKILL_NAMES];
2836:}
2837:
2838:/** Valid action statuses for state machine enforcement */
2839:export const VALID_ACTION_STATUSES = [
2840:  'proposed', 'pending_approval', 'approved', 'executing',
2841:  'completed', 'failed', 'rejected', 'blocked', 'skipped',
2842:] as const;
2843:
2844:export type ActionStatus = typeof VALID_ACTION_STATUSES[number];
2845:
2846:/** Legal state transitions */
2847:export const LEGAL_TRANSITIONS: Record<string, string[]> = {
2848:  proposed: ['pending_approval', 'approved', 'blocked', 'skipped', 'failed'],
2849:  pending_approval: ['approved', 'rejected'],
2850:  approved: ['executing'],
2851:  executing: ['completed', 'failed'],
2852:  // Terminal states: completed, failed, rejected, blocked, skipped — no transitions out
2853:};

 succeeded in 312ms:

48:
49:## 1. Scope + non-goals
50:
51:### 1.1 In scope (v1)
52:
53:- A **CRM Query Planner service** (`server/services/crmQueryPlanner/`) that classifies free-text CRM data questions into `canonical | live | hybrid | unsupported` and routes to the correct executor.
54:- A 4-stage planner pipeline: Intent Normalisation → Stage 1 (Registry Matcher) → Stage 2 (Plan Cache) → Stage 3 (LLM Fallback) → Stage 4 (Validator). Stages 1 and 2 never call the LLM.
55:- A first-class `canonicalQueryRegistry` with 8 v1 entries (§12).
56:- A canonical executor wrapping `canonicalDataService` (with extensions for non-metric reads).
57:- A live executor wrapping `ghlAdapter` read helpers, read-only by structural import restriction.
58:- One hybrid pattern: `canonical_base_with_live_filter` (§14).
59:- A result normaliser that emits `BriefStructuredResult` / `BriefApprovalCard` / `BriefErrorResult` per `shared/types/briefResultContract.ts`.
60:- One illustrative approval-card follow-up (`crm.send_email` targeting a result's contact IDs).
61:- Governance wiring through existing primitives: `llmRouter.routeCall`, `runCostBreaker`, `getProviderRateLimiter`, `withPrincipalContext`. No new governance primitives.
62:- Observability events + derived metrics (§17).
63:- One skill + one route (§18) for agents / chat surface to invoke.
64:
65:### 1.2 Out of scope (v1)
66:
67:- Hybrid patterns beyond `canonical_base_with_live_filter`. Other hybrid queries return `unsupported_query` with refinement suggestions.
68:- Semantic / fuzzy plan-cache matching. Exact-match normalised-intent only.
69:- Query Memory Layer surfacing. Logging is captured v1; the promotion dashboard is a follow-on feature with its own brief.
70:- Non-GHL live executors (HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive). Same planner layer, new adapters — later.
71:- Agent-facing `crm.schema_describe` diagnostic as a first-class skill. Schema is injected automatically at Stage 3.
72:- Export / saved-segment affordances from the result view (owned by Brief-surface branch).
73:- External MCP exposure of the planner.
74:- The universal Brief chat surface itself (separate branch).
75:- Free-text writes. All writes remain review-gated `crm.*` skills; the planner only emits read results and approval cards that point at existing structured skills.
76:
77:### 1.3 Non-goals (durable)
78:
79:These are the brief's non-goals, carried through:
80:
81:- Replacing canonical ingestion. Canonical stays the record of truth for everything ClientPulse and reporting depend on.
82:- An internal MCP protocol hop to call our own adapters. The planner calls services directly.
83:- Provider-specific UX. The planner is CRM-shaped; every surface stays provider-neutral.
84:- Parity with external Claude-Code + MCP setups. We absorb the category by offering better governance, not by cloning UX.
85:
86:---
87:
88:## 2. Closed decisions from brief §11
89:
90:The brief's §11 listed 12 open decisions for architect. This spec locks them as follows. Each decision is restated, the position is called out, and the rationale is given. Implementation follows these positions; any change requires updating the spec, not the code.
91:
92:| § | Decision | Position (v1) | Why |
93:|---|---|---|---|
94:| 11.1 | Initial `canonicalQueryRegistry` entries | 8 entries as listed in brief §6.1 / this spec §12.2 | ClientPulse already covers these conceptually via canonical rollups; adds no new data modelling; mirrors the questions real operators ask |
95:| 11.2 | Hybrid v1 posture | One pattern: `canonical_base_with_live_filter` | Rejects "unsupported" becoming the common error; keeps scope bounded |
96:| 11.3 | `crm.schema_describe` helper | **Not shipped v1.** Automatic schema injection at Stage 3 is sufficient | Every call paying an extra round-trip for schema lookup is the wrong default |
97:| 11.4 | Validator scope | Field existence + operator sanity + date-range sanity + entity-relation validity + aggregation compatibility | Brief's full list. Tighter = catches more bad plans before executor; not so tight it rejects legitimate edge cases |
98:| 11.5 | Per-subaccount rate-limit budget | **Deferred.** `getProviderRateLimiter('ghl')` already queues fairly; add budget only when real traffic signals abuse | Defensive budgets without signal are premature optimisation |
99:| 11.6 | LLM tier escalation rules | Haiku default; Sonnet escalation on `confidence < 0.6`, hybrid path, or large post-filter schema; single retry, no loop | Matches brief §5.3. Retry loops are where cost leaks happen |
100:| 11.7 | Caching posture | Plan cache (exact-match normalised intent, 60s TTL). Result cache deferred | Plans are more stable than results; result caching has low hit rate for free-text |
101:| 11.8 | Approval-card follow-up scope | One end-to-end example: `crm.send_email` approval card on a contact result set | Proves the wiring; more cards added after real UX signal |
102:| 11.9 | Observability surface | Full event set per brief §11.9 + `stageResolved` + derived metrics per §17 | Locked; feeds future Query Memory Layer |
103:| 11.10 | Contract drift check | Confirmed no drift: `shared/types/briefResultContract.ts` unchanged since 2026-04-22 merge | Re-verify at spec-reviewer time |
104:| 11.11 | Schema injection strategy | Entity-level summaries + top-N fields per entity + per-query filtering + per-subaccount cache keyed on `canonical_subaccount_mutations` version. Hard token budget: 2k default, 4k on escalation | Prevents bloat and hallucination |
105:| 11.12 | Query Memory Layer | **Logging only in v1.** Events shaped for future consumption; no surfacing | Surfacing is its own feature with its own brief |
106:
107:### 2.1 Decisions still open for implementation-time discovery
108:
109:These are *not* closed by this spec — they need real code context to resolve, and are flagged as `// TODO(spec-open-N)` comments in the implementation:
110:
111:1. The exact alias list per `canonicalQueryRegistry` entry. Seed list in §12.3 is a starting point — pressure test (brief §13) will expand it.
112:2. The exact schema-context compression ratio per provider. 2k default may need tuning once real schemas are tried.
113:3. The `confidence < 0.6` threshold for escalation — may need tuning against real Stage 3 output distributions.
114:
115:These do not block implementation; they are settled inside the first test pass.
116:
117:---
118:
119:## 3. Architectural overview
120:
121:The planner is a single service with four internal stages, three executors, and one normaliser. It sits below the Orchestrator in the capability hierarchy (Orchestrator owns capability routing; Planner owns data-query routing for CRM reads). Every surface that reads CRM data — Brief chat, agent tool call, stopgap Ask CRM panel — calls the planner; no surface calls the provider adapters directly.
122:
123:The planner is deterministic-first: Stages 1 and 2 never consult the LLM. The LLM is the Stage 3 fallback, invoked only when pattern matching and cache both miss. The validator is Stage 4 and is authoritative — LLM output is advisory. Each query resolves to exactly one validated QueryPlan; no parallel candidate plans.
124:
125:See §6 for type contracts and §7–§11 for per-stage behaviour.
126:
127:---
128:
129:## 4. Data flow — end-to-end
130:
131:```text
132:User / agent emits intent → Brief chat surface
133:                              ↓
134:                    POST /api/crm-query-planner/query
135:                    { rawIntent, briefContext: { orgId, subaccountId, userId, runId? } }
136:                              ↓
137:             ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐
138:             │  normaliseIntent(rawIntent)           │
139:             │  → NormalisedIntent { hash, tokens, …}│
140:             └───────────────────────────────────────┘
141:                              ↓
142:     ┌─────────────────────── Stage 1 ─────────────────────────┐
143:     │  canonicalQueryRegistry.lookupByAlias(normalised)        │
144:     │  hit → QueryPlan { source: 'canonical', stageResolved:1 }│
145:     │  miss → next                                             │
146:     └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
147:                              ↓
148:     ┌─────────────────────── Stage 2 ─────────────────────────┐
149:     │  planCache.get(normalised.hash, subaccountId)            │
150:     │  hit → QueryPlan (loaded, stageResolved: 2)              │
151:     │  miss → next                                             │
152:     └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
153:                              ↓
154:     ┌─────────────────────── Stage 3 ─────────────────────────┐
155:     │  schemaContext = buildSchemaContext(subaccountId, hints) │
156:     │  llmRouter.routeCall({                                   │
157:     │    task: 'crm_query_planner',                            │
158:     │    model: resolvePlannerTier(orgId, escalate:false),     │
159:     │    messages: [ promptWithSchemaAndIntent ],              │
160:     │    schema: DraftQueryPlanSchema                          │
161:     │  })                                                      │
162:     │  → DraftQueryPlan { confidence, canonicalCandidateKey, … }│
163:     │  if confidence < ESCALATION_THRESHOLD → one retry at     │
164:     │  escalated tier; no further retries                      │
165:     └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
166:                              ↓
167:     ┌─────────────────────── Stage 4 ─────────────────────────┐
168:     │  validateQueryPlan(draft, schemaContext, registry)       │
169:     │  • field / operator / entity / aggregation checks         │
170:     │  • canonical-precedence tie-breaker                      │
171:     │  • hybrid-pattern shape check                            │
172:     │  pass → QueryPlan { stageResolved: 3, validated: true }  │
173:     │  fail → BriefErrorResult { code: 'ambiguous_intent' }    │
174:     │  On pass: planCache.set(normalised.hash, plan)           │
175:     └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
176:                              ↓
177:              ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
178:              ↓               ↓               ↓
179:     canonicalExecutor   liveExecutor   hybridExecutor
180:              ↓               ↓               ↓
181:              └───────────────┼───────────────┘
182:                              ↓
183:             ┌────────────────┴───────────────┐
184:             │  resultNormaliser(execResult)  │
185:             │  → BriefStructuredResult       │
186:             │    + optional BriefApprovalCard│
187:             └────────────────────────────────┘
188:                              ↓
189:                        Response to caller
190:```
191:
192:Emitted artefacts match `shared/types/briefResultContract.ts` exactly. The response shape is one `BriefStructuredResult` plus zero-to-N `BriefApprovalCard` suggestions, or one `BriefErrorResult` if any stage failed.
193:
194:---
195:
196:## 5. File / module layout
197:
198:Every file below is new unless marked `[existing — extend]`. All TypeScript; all pure modules have a sibling `*Pure.test.ts` per the repo convention.
199:
200:```
201:server/
202:├── services/
203:│   └── crmQueryPlanner/
204:│       ├── index.ts                            # Public facade: runQuery(intent, context)
205:│       ├── crmQueryPlannerService.ts           # Orchestration (4-stage pipeline)
206:│       ├── normaliseIntentPure.ts              # §7 — shared normaliser utility
207:│       ├── registryMatcherPure.ts              # §8 — Stage 1 deterministic matcher
208:│       ├── planCache.ts                        # §9 — Stage 2 in-process cache
209:│       ├── planCachePure.ts                    # §9 — pure key / eviction logic
210:│       ├── llmPlanner.ts                       # §10 — Stage 3 LLM fallback
211:│       ├── llmPlannerPromptPure.ts             # §10 — prompt assembly (pure)
212:│       ├── validatePlanPure.ts                 # §11 — Stage 4 validator
213:│       ├── schemaContextService.ts             # §11.11 — schema summarisation + cache
214:│       ├── schemaContextPure.ts                # §11.11 — compression / filtering (pure)
215:│       ├── executors/
216:│       │   ├── canonicalExecutor.ts            # §12 — registry-backed
217:│       │   ├── canonicalQueryRegistry.ts       # §12 — registry of 8 v1 entries
218:│       │   ├── liveExecutor.ts                 # §13 — ghlAdapter-backed, read-only
219:│       │   └── hybridExecutor.ts               # §14 — canonical_base_with_live_filter only
220:│       ├── resultNormaliser.ts                 # §15 — QueryPlan + executor result → Brief artefact
221:│       ├── resultNormaliserPure.ts             # §15 — pure envelope construction
222:│       ├── approvalCardGeneratorPure.ts        # §15 — follow-up action detection
223:│       ├── plannerEvents.ts                    # §17 — structured event emission
224:│       └── __tests__/
225:│           ├── normaliseIntentPure.test.ts
226:│           ├── registryMatcherPure.test.ts
227:│           ├── planCachePure.test.ts
228:│           ├── llmPlannerPromptPure.test.ts
229:│           ├── validatePlanPure.test.ts
230:│           ├── schemaContextPure.test.ts
231:│           ├── resultNormaliserPure.test.ts
232:│           ├── approvalCardGeneratorPure.test.ts
233:│           ├── canonicalQueryRegistry.test.ts
234:│           ├── liveExecutor.test.ts            # §13.6 — plan-to-provider translation + rate-limit lifecycle (mocked ghlReadHelpers)
235:│           ├── hybridExecutor.test.ts          # §14.4 — canonical+live merge + 10-call cap (mocked executors)
236:│           ├── crmQueryPlannerService.test.ts  # §20.2 — orchestration with mocked registry/cache/llmRouter/executors/runCostBreaker
237:│           └── integration.test.ts             # §20.2 — RLS isolation only; uses existing rls.context-propagation.test.ts harness
238:├── services/
239:│   ├── canonicalDataService.ts                 # [existing — extend] add non-metric reads
240:│   └── systemPnlService.ts                     # [existing — extend in P3] add planner metrics subsection
241:├── routes/
242:│   └── crmQueryPlanner.ts                      # §18 — POST /api/crm-query-planner/query
243:├── config/
244:│   └── actionRegistry.ts                       # [existing — extend] register `crm.query` skill
245:└── db/schema/
246:    └── (no schema changes in v1)               # Plan cache is in-memory; see §9
247:
248:scripts/
249:└── verify-crm-query-planner-read-only.sh      # §13.3 / §16.6 — static gate: no write-helper imports under executors/
250:
251:shared/
252:└── types/
253:    ├── briefResultContract.ts                  # [existing — no changes]
254:    └── crmQueryPlanner.ts                      # §6 — QueryPlan, registry, events, mapOperatorForWire
255:
256:client/
257:└── src/pages/
258:    └── SystemPnlPage.tsx                       # [existing — extend in P3] render planner metrics subsection
259:
260:tasks/builds/crm-query-planner/
261:└── pressure-test-results.md                    # §20.3 — created during P2 pressure-test pass
262:```
263:
264:### 5.1 Reasons for this layout
265:
266:- **Pure / impure split** mirrors the rest of the server. Every planner stage's decision logic is pure (`*Pure.ts`) and unit-tested in isolation; the imperative wrappers (adapter calls, cache mutations, router calls) own side effects.
267:- **`crmQueryPlanner/` subdirectory** keeps the 18 new files together without polluting `server/services/` root. The pattern matches `server/services/workspaceHealth/` (eight detector files under one dir).
268:- **Registry under `executors/`** because the registry is only consumed by the canonical executor and Stage 1 matcher. Other registries that cross broader surfaces (e.g. `actionRegistry`) live in `server/config/`; this one doesn't.
269:- **No new DB tables v1.** Plan cache is in-process (§9). Observability events flow into existing run-trace infrastructure (§17).
270:- **Client changes scoped to P3.** P1/P2 add no client files. P3 extends the existing `SystemPnlPage.tsx` with the planner-metrics subsection (§19 P3). The Brief surface branch (separate branch) renders `BriefStructuredResult` already; the planner produces exactly that.
271:
272:---
273:
274:## 6. Type contracts
275:
276:All types defined in `shared/types/crmQueryPlanner.ts` so both server and (future) client consume the same shapes. TypeScript strict mode assumed (already set repo-wide).
277:
278:### 6.1 Normalised intent
279:
280:```ts
281:export interface NormalisedIntent {
282:  hash: string;                    // stable key for cache + registry lookups
283:  tokens: string[];                // lowercased, stop-word stripped, synonym-collapsed
284:  rawIntent: string;               // preserved for logging + error responses
285:}
286:```
287:
288:Purity: `normaliseIntent` returns only these fields — no clock stamp, no side-effects (see §7.4). If the service layer needs a diagnostic timestamp (e.g. for cache-freshness logs), it is stamped outside the pure function at the call-site.
289:
290:**Hash derivation:** `sha256(tokens.join(' ')).slice(0, 16)`. Sixteen hex chars = 64 bits of entropy, collision-safe for any realistic cache size.
291:
292:### 6.2 QueryPlan
293:
294:```ts
295:export type QuerySource = 'canonical' | 'live' | 'hybrid';
296:
297:// Matches the wire contract `BriefResultSource` exactly. Unsupported queries
298:// never reach the executor — the planner surfaces them as a `BriefErrorResult`
299:// with `errorCode: 'unsupported_query'` (see §15.1). Keeping `QuerySource` tight
300:// prevents §15.2 from ever copying an invalid wire value into a
301:// `BriefStructuredResult.source` field.
302:
303:export type QueryIntentClass =
304:  | 'list_entities'
305:  | 'count_entities'
306:  | 'aggregate'
307:  | 'lookup'
308:  | 'trend_request'
309:  | 'segment_request'
310:  | 'unsupported';  // planner-internal — never emitted on the wire; routes to `BriefErrorResult`
311:
312:export type PrimaryEntity =
313:  | 'contacts'
314:  | 'opportunities'
315:  | 'appointments'
316:  | 'conversations'
317:  | 'revenue'
318:  | 'tasks';
319:
320:// Matches the wire contract `BriefResultEntityType` exactly (minus 'runs' and
321:// 'other', which are non-CRM surfaces owned by other features). Tag-related
322:// queries use `primaryEntity: 'contacts'` with tag grouping — see the
323:// `contacts.count_by_tag` registry entry in §12.2.
324:
325:export type StageResolved = 1 | 2 | 3;
326:
327:export interface QueryFilter {
328:  field: string;
329:  operator: 'eq' | 'ne' | 'in' | 'nin' | 'gt' | 'gte' | 'lt' | 'lte'
330:          | 'contains' | 'starts_with' | 'is_null' | 'is_not_null'
331:          | 'between';
332:  value: unknown;
333:  humanLabel: string;              // for filtersApplied rendering
334:}
335:
336:// Wire translation — `BriefResultFilter.operator` is a string per the
337:// committed contract, but its doc-comment documents the set
338:// `eq | neq | gt | gte | lt | lte | in | contains | between | exists`.
339:// Three planner-internal operators don't round-trip cleanly:
340://   - `ne`          → 'neq'         (spelling drift)
341://   - `is_null`     → 'exists' with value=false
342://   - `is_not_null` → 'exists' with value=true
343:// `nin` and `starts_with` are emitted as-is (wire type is `string`, additive);
344:// consumers ignore unknown values per the contract's "additive changes" rule.
345:// The result normaliser (§15.2) MUST route every `QueryFilter.operator` through
346:// `mapOperatorForWire` before populating `BriefResultFilter.operator`; no other
347:// code path should copy operators through.
348:
349:export function mapOperatorForWire(op: QueryFilter['operator']):
350:  { operator: string; value?: unknown } {
351:  switch (op) {
352:    case 'ne':          return { operator: 'neq' };
353:    case 'is_null':     return { operator: 'exists', value: false };
354:    case 'is_not_null': return { operator: 'exists', value: true };
355:    default:            return { operator: op };
356:  }
357:}
358:
359:export interface QueryPlan {
360:  source: QuerySource;
361:  intentClass: QueryIntentClass;
362:  primaryEntity: PrimaryEntity;
363:  relatedEntities?: PrimaryEntity[];
364:  filters: QueryFilter[];
365:  sort?: Array<{ field: string; direction: 'asc' | 'desc' }>;
366:  limit: number;
367:  projection?: string[];
368:  aggregation?: {
369:    type: 'count' | 'sum' | 'avg' | 'group_by';
370:    field?: string;
371:    groupBy?: string[];
372:  };
373:  dateContext?: {
374:    kind: 'relative' | 'absolute';
375:    from?: string;                 // ISO 8601
376:    to?: string;
377:    description?: string;
378:  };
379:  canonicalCandidateKey: string | null;    // registry key if canonical-promotable
380:  confidence: number;              // 0..1; always 1.0 for Stages 1 & 2
381:  stageResolved: StageResolved;
382:  hybridPattern?: 'canonical_base_with_live_filter';  // v1 sole pattern
383:  costPreview: BriefCostPreview;   // from shared/types/briefResultContract.ts
384:  validated: true;                 // literal — present only after Stage 4
385:}
386:
387:export interface DraftQueryPlan extends Omit<QueryPlan, 'validated' | 'stageResolved'> {
388:  // Shape Stage 3 emits before validation
389:  clarificationNeeded?: boolean;
390:  clarificationPrompt?: string;
391:}
392:```
393:
394:**Why the `validated: true` literal:** prevents unvalidated draft plans from reaching the executor by TypeScript compile-time check. The executor signature is `(plan: QueryPlan) => Promise<ExecutorResult>` — a `DraftQueryPlan` cannot be passed in without explicit widening, which no production code path does.
395:
396:### 6.3 Canonical query registry
397:
398:```ts
399:export interface CanonicalQueryRegistryEntry {
400:  key: string;                                   // 'contacts.inactive_over_days'
401:  aliases: string[];                             // normalised-intent strings Stage 1 matches against
402:  primaryEntity: PrimaryEntity;
403:  requiredCapabilities: string[];                // e.g. ['canonical.contacts.read'] — enforced by canonicalExecutor (§12.1)
404:  handler: CanonicalQueryHandler;
405:  description: string;                           // for docs and diagnostics
406:
407:  // Parses free-text args out of the normalised intent into typed filters /
408:  // dateContext / limit / sort / projection. Pure. Returns null if the intent
409:  // is an alias match but the args could not be parsed — caller falls through
410:  // to Stage 2 (see §8.3). Invoked only by the Stage 1 matcher.
411:  parseArgs?: (intent: NormalisedIntent) => ParsedArgs | null;
412:}
413:
414:export interface ParsedArgs {
415:  filters?: QueryFilter[];
416:  dateContext?: QueryPlan['dateContext'];
417:  limit?: number;
418:  sort?: QueryPlan['sort'];
419:  projection?: string[];
420:}
421:
422:export interface CanonicalQueryHandlerArgs {
423:  orgId: string;
424:  subaccountId: string;
425:  filters: QueryFilter[];
426:  dateContext?: QueryPlan['dateContext'];
427:  limit: number;
428:  sort?: QueryPlan['sort'];
429:  projection?: string[];
430:}
431:
432:export type CanonicalQueryHandler = (
433:  args: CanonicalQueryHandlerArgs
434:) => Promise<ExecutorResult>;
435:
436:export type CanonicalQueryRegistry = Readonly<Record<string, CanonicalQueryRegistryEntry>>;
437:```
438:
439:### 6.4 Executor result (internal — not on the wire)
440:
441:```ts
442:export interface ExecutorResult {
443:  rows: Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
444:  rowCount: number;
445:  truncated: boolean;
446:  truncationReason?: 'result_limit' | 'cost_limit' | 'time_limit';
447:  actualCostCents: number;
448:  source: QuerySource;             // echoed for provenance
449:  providerLatencyMs?: number;      // for live / hybrid only
450:}
451:```
452:
453:### 6.5 Plan cache entry
454:
455:```ts
456:export interface PlanCacheEntry {
457:  plan: QueryPlan;
458:  cachedAt: number;                // epoch ms
459:  subaccountId: string;            // part of cache key
460:  hits: number;                    // for Query Memory Layer later
461:}
462:```
463:
464:### 6.6 Planner events
465:
466:```ts
467:export type PlannerEventKind =
468:  | 'planner.stage1_matched'
469:  | 'planner.stage1_missed'
470:  | 'planner.stage2_cache_hit'
471:  | 'planner.stage2_cache_miss'
472:  | 'planner.stage3_parse_started'
473:  | 'planner.stage3_parse_completed'
474:  | 'planner.stage3_escalated'
475:  | 'planner.validation_failed'
476:  | 'planner.classified'
477:  | 'planner.executor_dispatched'
478:  | 'planner.canonical_promoted'
479:  | 'planner.result_emitted'
480:  | 'planner.error_emitted';
481:
482:export interface PlannerEvent<K extends PlannerEventKind = PlannerEventKind> {
483:  kind: K;
484:  at: number;
485:  orgId: string;
486:  subaccountId: string;
487:  runId?: string;
488:  intentHash: string;
489:  payload: Record<string, unknown>;   // per-kind shape documented in §17
490:}
491:```
492:
493:All emitted through `plannerEvents.emit(event)` which forwards to structured logs and the run-trace event stream (§17).
494:
495:---
496:
497:## 7. Intent normalisation
498:
499:**File:** `server/services/crmQueryPlanner/normaliseIntentPure.ts`
500:
501:Shared utility consumed by **both** Stage 1 (matcher) and Stage 2 (cache). Single source of truth for how raw intent text maps to a `NormalisedIntent` — enforced by both stages importing this function only.
502:
503:### 7.1 Algorithm
504:
505:```ts
506:export function normaliseIntent(rawIntent: string): NormalisedIntent {
507:  // 1. lowercase
508:  // 2. strip punctuation except digits and '-' in "30-day" style
509:  // 3. collapse whitespace to single spaces
510:  // 4. tokenise on whitespace
511:  // 5. remove stop words (see STOP_WORDS below)
512:  // 6. apply synonym canonicalisation (see SYNONYMS below)
513:  // 7. sort-stabilise date literals: "last 30 days" / "30 days ago" / "past month" → "last_30d"
514:  // 8. compute hash = sha256(tokens.join(' ')).slice(0,16)
515:}
516:```
517:
518:### 7.2 Stop words (v1 list)
519:
520:`a, an, the, is, are, was, were, to, of, for, on, in, at, by, with, me, my, our, show, get, find, list`
521:
522:Conservative list. Intent-bearing words (`all`, `only`, `without`, `not`) are kept.
523:
524:### 7.3 Synonyms (v1 map)
525:
526:```ts
527:const SYNONYMS: Record<string, string> = {
528:  'deals': 'opportunities',
529:  'deal': 'opportunities',
530:  'leads': 'contacts',
531:  'lead': 'contacts',
532:  'customer': 'contacts',
533:  'customers': 'contacts',
534:  'client': 'contacts',
535:  'clients': 'contacts',
536:  'pipeline': 'opportunities',
537:  'inactive': 'stale',
538:  'dormant': 'stale',
539:  'idle': 'stale',
540:  'upcoming': 'future',
541:  // ... seed list; pressure test expands
542:};
543:```
544:
545:The synonym map is the primary lever for Stage 1 hit rate. The pressure test in §20.3 identifies missing synonyms; they're added here.
546:
547:### 7.4 Determinism invariants
548:
549:- Same input → same output. No randomness, no clock dependency (the `normalisedAt` field on `NormalisedIntent` is a diagnostic; not part of the hash).
550:- Pure function. No side effects. No external state. Enables full unit-test coverage.
551:- Order of synonym application is deterministic (single pass in insertion order). No rule can override a prior rule within the same call.
552:
553:### 7.5 Test plan (pure)
554:
555:`normaliseIntentPure.test.ts` — minimum 15 cases:
556:
557:- 3 identity cases (already-normalised input)
558:- 3 whitespace / casing variants produce identical hash
559:- 3 synonym replacements work bidirectionally (original + alias produce same hash)
560:- 3 stop-word stripping cases
561:- 3 date-literal canonicalisation cases
562:
563:---
564:
565:## 8. Stage 1 — Registry-backed matcher
566:
567:**File:** `server/services/crmQueryPlanner/registryMatcherPure.ts`
568:**Stage contract:** given a `NormalisedIntent` + the `canonicalQueryRegistry`, return a `QueryPlan` or `null`.
569:
570:### 8.1 Algorithm
571:
572:```ts
573:export function matchRegistryEntry(
574:  intent: NormalisedIntent,
575:  registry: CanonicalQueryRegistry
576:): QueryPlan | null {
577:  // 1. Build alias index (memoised; see §8.2)
578:  // 2. Look up intent.hash in alias index → registry key
579:  // 3. If found, construct a QueryPlan with:
580:  //    - source: 'canonical'
581:  //    - stageResolved: 1
582:  //    - canonicalCandidateKey: <registry key>
583:  //    - confidence: 1.0
584:  //    - validated: true  (Stage 1 hits skip Stage 4 — see §8.3)
585:  //    - filters/dateContext: extracted from the raw intent via per-entry parsers
586:  // 4. If not found, return null
587:}
588:```
589:
590:### 8.2 Alias index construction
591:
592:```ts
593:function buildAliasIndex(
594:  registry: CanonicalQueryRegistry
595:): Map<string, string /* registry key */> {
596:  const index = new Map();
597:  for (const [key, entry] of Object.entries(registry)) {
598:    for (const alias of entry.aliases) {
599:      const aliasHash = normaliseIntent(alias).hash;
600:      if (index.has(aliasHash)) {
601:        throw new Error(`Alias collision: "${alias}" in ${key} conflicts with ${index.get(aliasHash)}`);
602:      }
603:      index.set(aliasHash, key);
604:    }
605:  }
606:  return index;
607:}
608:```
609:
610:Built once at module load and cached in a module-level `WeakMap<CanonicalQueryRegistry, Map<string, string>>`. Collision throw at build prevents two entries claiming the same normalised alias (caught at startup, not runtime).
611:
612:### 8.3 Stage 1 hits run a reduced validator subset
613:
614:A Stage 1 match is mostly structurally valid — the registry entry names the `primaryEntity`, the handler is typed, and the intent was deterministically matched. Most of Stage 4's rules (entity existence, operator sanity on registry-fixed filters, entity-relation validity, aggregation compatibility, hybrid-pattern check, canonical-precedence tie-breaker) are trivially satisfied and skipped.
615:
616:But the `parseArgs` hook extracts filters / projection / sort / dateContext from free-text intent — those *are* user-derived and MUST pass:
617:
618:- **Rule 2 — field existence** for any `parseArgs`-produced `filters[].field`, `sort[].field`, and `projection[]` entry, against the caller's current schemaContext.
619:- **Rule 3 — operator sanity** for any `parseArgs`-produced filter.
620:- **Rule 9 — projection overlap** (read-permission check) for any `parseArgs`-produced projection field.
621:
622:These three rules run via `validatePlanPure` in "stage1_mode" (the validator takes a mode flag that scopes it to the subset above). If any fail, the Stage 1 result is discarded and the caller falls through to Stage 2. If `parseArgs` itself returns `null`, Stage 1 also falls through.
623:
624:Stage 1 is still the zero-LLM fast path; the reduced-validator-subset is pure, cheap, and closes the gap that would otherwise let a badly-parsed alias dispatch a plan referencing a nonexistent field.
625:
626:### 8.4 Test plan (pure)
627:
628:`registryMatcherPure.test.ts`:
629:
630:- For every registry entry (§12.2), every listed alias (35 total) produces a hit. One test case per alias.
631:- Alias-collision detection fires at index build time
632:- Empty / malformed intent returns null
633:- Intent that normalises to a non-aliased hash returns null
634:- QueryPlan emitted has `validated: true` and `stageResolved: 1`
635:
636:Total: ~40 cases (35 alias hits + ~5 edge cases). The §20.1 summary row uses the same count; keep the two in sync when the alias list grows.
637:
638:---
639:
640:## 9. Stage 2 — Plan cache
641:
642:**Files:** `server/services/crmQueryPlanner/planCache.ts` + `planCachePure.ts`
643:**Stage contract:** given a `NormalisedIntent` hash + subaccountId, return a cached `QueryPlan` or `null`.
644:
645:### 9.1 Cache shape
646:
647:In-process `Map<string /* cacheKey */, PlanCacheEntry>`. Key format:
648:
649:```ts
650:const cacheKey = (hash: string, subaccountId: string) => `${subaccountId}:${hash}`;
651:```
652:
653:Subaccount scoping prevents cross-subaccount cache pollution (two subaccounts with identical intents may have different schema shapes, so plans differ).
654:
655:### 9.2 Eviction policy
656:
657:- **TTL:** 60 seconds from `cachedAt`. Mirrors `crmLiveDataService` picker cache convention.
658:- **Max entries:** 500 per process. LRU eviction on overflow. Matches picker cache.
659:- **Overwrite:** most-recent-wins. A successful validation always overwrites any prior entry for the same key.
660:- **Invalidation on schema change:** **not in v1.** The 60s TTL is short enough that schema-drift will flush through within one cache generation. Schema-change-driven flush (listening for `canonical_subaccount_mutations` version bumps) is tracked in the Deferred Items section as a v2 concern.
661:
662:### 9.3 Cache-write rules
663:
664:- **Only validated plans** (`stageResolved === 3`) are written to cache. Stage 1 hits are already free and don't need caching. Stage 2 hits don't re-cache (the entry is already present).
665:- Cache write happens inside the validator's success path, not inside the LLM planner, so a Stage 3 plan that fails validation is never cached.
666:
667:### 9.4 Future alignment with Query Memory Layer (brief §11.12)
668:
669:The `PlanCacheEntry` includes a `hits` counter incremented on every cache hit. Combined with eviction, this forms the dataset the Query Memory Layer will consume for "top N live queries → canonical promotion candidates" analytics (brief §11.12). No v1 surfacing — the counter exists solely so v2 has the data.
670:
671:### 9.5 Test plan (pure)
672:
673:`planCachePure.test.ts`:
674:
675:- Cache put + get round-trip produces identical plan
676:- Entry expiry at `cachedAt + TTL_MS` returns null
677:- LRU eviction fires at 501st entry; oldest-accessed entry evicted
678:- Overwrite updates `cachedAt` and resets `hits`
679:- Cross-subaccount isolation: same hash, different subaccount → two entries, no collision
680:
681:---
682:
683:## 10. Stage 3 — LLM planner
684:
685:**Files:** `server/services/crmQueryPlanner/llmPlanner.ts` + `llmPlannerPromptPure.ts`
686:**Stage contract:** given a `NormalisedIntent` + `SchemaContext` + `BriefCostPreview` budget, return a `DraftQueryPlan` or throw.
687:
688:### 10.1 Router call
689:
690:```ts
691:const result = await llmRouter.routeCall({
692:  task: 'crm_query_planner',
693:  context: {
694:    sourceType: 'system',
695:    sourceId: 'crm_query_planner',
696:    featureTag: 'crm-query-planner',
697:    systemCallerPolicy: 'strict',
698:    orgId,
699:    subaccountId,
700:    runId,
701:  },
702:  model: resolvePlannerTier(orgId, escalate),   // §10.3
703:  messages: buildPrompt(intent, schemaContext), // §10.2
704:  postProcess: DraftQueryPlanSchema.parse,      // zod validation of JSON
705:  abortSignal,
706:});
707:```
708:
709:Routing through `llmRouter.routeCall` is **mandatory** — the repo-wide gate (`verify-no-direct-adapter-calls.sh`) blocks direct provider imports. The planner is not allowed to bypass this.
710:
711:### 10.2 Prompt shape
712:
713:`buildPrompt(intent, schemaContext)` is a pure function in `llmPlannerPromptPure.ts`. Structure:
714:
715:```
716:SYSTEM:
717:  You are a CRM Query Planner. Convert the user's intent into a structured QueryPlan.
718:
719:  Rules:
720:  - Prefer canonical sources when the question matches one of the canonical registry keys below.
721:  - For hybrid questions, set source='hybrid' ONLY if it matches the v1 pattern:
722:    one canonical base + one live filter refinement.
723:  - For questions outside the registry and not matching the hybrid pattern,
724:    set source='live'.
725:  - For questions that cannot be expressed within the available entities/fields,
726:    set source='unsupported' and include a clarificationPrompt.
727:
728:  Always include a confidence score (0..1). Low confidence triggers escalation.
729:
730:  CANONICAL REGISTRY:
731:  {{registryKeysWithDescriptions}}
732:
733:  AVAILABLE SCHEMA (filtered for relevance):
734:  {{schemaContext.compressed}}
735:
736:  RESPONSE FORMAT (JSON):
737:  {{DraftQueryPlanSchema}}
738:
739:USER:
740:  {{intent.rawIntent}}
741:```
742:
743:Prompt is assembled as a single system + user message pair. No few-shot examples in v1 (keeps tokens lean; pressure test §20.3 determines if any are needed).
744:
745:### 10.3 Tier resolution
746:
747:```ts
748:function resolvePlannerTier(orgId: string, escalate: boolean): ModelTier {
749:  const config = loadOrgPlannerConfig(orgId);  // org-level override, else system default
750:  return escalate ? config.escalation_tier : config.default_tier;
751:}
752:```
753:
754:**System defaults** (in `systemSettings` table or hardcoded constants for v1):
755:
756:```ts
757:{
758:  crm_query_planner_default_tier: 'haiku',
759:  crm_query_planner_escalation_tier: 'sonnet',
760:  crm_query_planner_confidence_threshold: 0.6,
761:}
762:```
763:
764:Per-org overrides live in `organisation_hierarchies.operational_config.crm_query_planner` if present.
765:
766:### 10.4 Escalation (single retry, never a loop)
767:
768:```ts
769:const draft1 = await runLlmPlanner({ ..., escalate: false });
770:if (draft1.confidence >= threshold || draft1.source === 'canonical' /* always escalate-safe */) {
771:  return draft1;
772:}
773:const draft2 = await runLlmPlanner({ ..., escalate: true });
774:return draft2;  // No further retry — validator will reject if still bad
775:```
776:
777:Hybrid-path detection also auto-escalates:
778:
779:```ts
780:if (detectLikelyHybrid(intent, schemaContext)) {
781:  return runLlmPlanner({ ..., escalate: true });  // Skip Stage 3 at default tier
782:}
783:```
784:
785:`detectLikelyHybrid` is a simple heuristic (intent references both a canonical-known entity and a live-only field). Cheap pre-check; avoids an escalation round-trip.
786:
787:### 10.5 Test plan
788:
789:Pure tests in `llmPlannerPromptPure.test.ts`:
790:
791:- Prompt includes all registry keys
792:- Prompt includes schema context verbatim
793:- Prompt truncates raw intent at 2k chars (safety)
794:- Registry descriptions have no placeholder-injection risk
795:
796:Integration tests for `llmPlanner.ts` mock `llmRouter.routeCall` and assert:
797:
798:- Confidence < threshold → escalation call fires
799:- Confidence ≥ threshold → no escalation
800:- Hybrid heuristic → escalates on first call
801:- Router errors surface as `BriefErrorResult` with `errorCode: 'ambiguous_intent'`
802:
803:---
804:
805:## 11. Stage 4 — Validator
806:
807:**File:** `server/services/crmQueryPlanner/validatePlanPure.ts`
808:**Stage contract:** given a `DraftQueryPlan` + `SchemaContext` + `CanonicalQueryRegistry`, return a validated `QueryPlan` or `ValidationError`.
809:
810:### 11.1 Authority
811:
812:The validator is **authoritative**. LLM output is advisory. This is not enforced by convention — it's enforced by the TypeScript literal `validated: true` on `QueryPlan` (§6.2). A `DraftQueryPlan` cannot reach any executor without passing through `validatePlan`.
813:
814:### 11.2 Validation rules
815:
816:Per brief §11.4, in order:
817:
818:1. **Entity existence** — `primaryEntity` is in `PrimaryEntity` enum; resolved via schemaContext.
819:2. **Field existence** — every `filter.field`, `sort.field`, `projection` field, `aggregation.field`, `aggregation.groupBy` entry exists on `primaryEntity` per the subaccount's schemaContext.
820:3. **Operator sanity** — each filter's `operator` is valid for the field's type (e.g. no `gt` on a string field unless it's date-ish).
821:4. **Date-range sanity** — `dateContext.from < dateContext.to` when both present.
822:5. **Entity-relation validity** — e.g. filtering on `opportunity.stage` requires `primaryEntity === 'opportunities'`.
823:6. **Aggregation compatibility** — `sum`/`avg` only on numeric fields; `group_by` fields must exist on `primaryEntity`.
824:7. **Hybrid pattern check** — if `source === 'hybrid'`, plan must match `canonical_base_with_live_filter` shape:
825:   - `canonicalCandidateKey` is non-null and in the registry
826:   - Exactly one filter is a "live" filter (live-only field per schemaContext)
827:   - Other filters are canonical-resolvable
828:   - `limit` ≤ canonical base's native limit
829:   - `hybridPattern` field set to `'canonical_base_with_live_filter'`.
830:8. **Canonical-precedence tie-breaker** — applies only when `source === 'live'` AND `canonicalCandidateKey` is non-null and registry-valid. Three cases:
831:   - **Zero live-only filters present** → promote `source` to `'canonical'`, drop `canonicalCandidateKey` from draft (no-op), log `planner.canonical_promoted`, dispatch via canonical executor.
832:   - **Live-only filters present AND plan matches `canonical_base_with_live_filter` shape** → promote to `source: 'hybrid'` with `hybridPattern: 'canonical_base_with_live_filter'`, log `planner.canonical_promoted` with `toSource: 'hybrid'`, dispatch via hybrid executor (once P3 ships — see §19.1 P2 rejection rule).
833:   - **Live-only filters present AND plan does NOT match the hybrid shape** → keep as `source: 'live'`, no promotion. The filters the user asked for are preserved.
834:
835:   The rule never silently strips filters. Dropping a user-specified filter would change query semantics — which is a correctness bug, not a tie-breaker.
836:9. **Projection overlap** — `projection` fields must be a subset of fields permitted for `primaryEntity` on the caller's principal context (read-permission check).
837:
838:### 11.3 Validation failure
839:
840:On any rule failing:
841:
842:```ts
843:throw new ValidationError({
844:  rejectedRule: 'field_existence' | 'operator_sanity' | ...,
845:  rejectedValue: <the offending value>,
846:  suggestions: generateRefinementSuggestions(draft, rejectedRule),
847:});
848:```
849:
850:The caller (`crmQueryPlannerService`) catches `ValidationError` and emits `BriefErrorResult { errorCode: 'ambiguous_intent', message, suggestions }`. **No retry.** No second LLM call. The refinement is the user's next Brief.
851:
852:### 11.4 On validation success
853:
854:`validatePlanPure` is strictly pure — it returns a validated `QueryPlan` (with `stageResolved: 3` and `validated: true`) and performs no I/O or event emission. The imperative wrapper in `crmQueryPlannerService.ts` handles side effects after the pure call returns success:
855:
856:- Writes the plan to `planCache` (§9).
857:- Emits `planner.classified` event with full plan shape.
858:- Dispatches to the executor.
859:
860:Keeping cache writes and event emission out of `validatePlanPure` mirrors the repo's pure/impure split and keeps `validatePlanPure.test.ts` hermetic (no mock cache, no mock event bus).
861:
862:For Stage 1 "stage1_mode" calls (§8.3), the service does NOT cache the plan — Stage 1 results are deterministic functions of `(registry, intent)` so caching adds no value. Only Stage 3 successes are cached.
863:
864:### 11.5 Test plan (pure)
865:
866:`validatePlanPure.test.ts` — at least 20 cases:
867:
868:- One pass case per rule (rule's check succeeds, plan emerges validated)
869:- One fail case per rule (rule's check fails, `ValidationError` thrown with correct `rejectedRule`)
870:- Canonical-precedence tie-breaker: live plan with valid `canonicalCandidateKey` promotes to canonical
871:- Hybrid-pattern check: matching plan passes; extra filter → rejected; no `canonicalCandidateKey` → rejected
872:- Cross-subaccount field leak attempt (projection includes field from another subaccount's schema) → rejected
873:
874:---
875:
876:## 12. Canonical executor + `canonicalQueryRegistry`
877:
878:**Files:** `server/services/crmQueryPlanner/executors/canonicalExecutor.ts` + `canonicalQueryRegistry.ts`
879:
880:### 12.1 Executor contract
881:
882:```ts
883:export async function executeCanonical(
884:  plan: QueryPlan,
885:  context: ExecutorContext
886:): Promise<ExecutorResult> {
887:  if (plan.source !== 'canonical') {
888:    throw new Error('canonicalExecutor dispatched with non-canonical plan');
889:  }
890:  if (!plan.canonicalCandidateKey) {
891:    throw new Error('canonical plan missing canonicalCandidateKey');
892:  }
893:  const entry = canonicalQueryRegistry[plan.canonicalCandidateKey];
894:  if (!entry) {
895:    throw new Error(`registry key not found: ${plan.canonicalCandidateKey}`);
896:  }
897:  // Per-entry capability check — enforced at dispatch, not at the route.
898:  // `context.callerCapabilities` is hydrated from the caller's permission
899:  // context (see §16.4). Missing any required capability surfaces as
900:  // `BriefErrorResult { errorCode: 'missing_permission' }` upstream.
901:  for (const cap of entry.requiredCapabilities) {
902:    if (!context.callerCapabilities.includes(cap)) {
903:      throw new MissingPermissionError(cap);
904:    }
905:  }
906:  return entry.handler({
907:    orgId: context.orgId,
908:    subaccountId: context.subaccountId,
909:    filters: plan.filters,
910:    dateContext: plan.dateContext,
911:    limit: plan.limit,
912:    sort: plan.sort,
913:    projection: plan.projection,
914:  });
915:}
916:```
917:
918:`MissingPermissionError` is a small service-layer error thrown by the executor and caught by `crmQueryPlannerService.ts` → emitted as `BriefErrorResult { errorCode: 'missing_permission', message }`.
919:
920:### 12.2 Registry — 8 v1 entries
921:
922:```ts
923:export const canonicalQueryRegistry: CanonicalQueryRegistry = Object.freeze({
924:  'contacts.inactive_over_days': {
925:    key: 'contacts.inactive_over_days',
926:    primaryEntity: 'contacts',
927:    aliases: [
928:      'inactive contacts',
929:      'stale contacts',
930:      'contacts no activity',
931:      'contacts without activity',
932:      'dormant contacts',
933:    ],
934:    requiredCapabilities: ['canonical.contacts.read'],
935:    description: 'Contacts with no activity since N days ago',
936:    handler: contactsInactiveOverDaysHandler,
937:  },
938:  'accounts.at_risk_band': {
939:    key: 'accounts.at_risk_band',
940:    primaryEntity: 'contacts',
941:    aliases: [
942:      'at risk accounts',
943:      'churn risk',
944:      'accounts likely to churn',
945:      'red accounts',
946:      'yellow accounts',
947:    ],
948:    requiredCapabilities: ['canonical.contacts.read', 'clientpulse.health_snapshots.read'],
949:    description: 'ClientPulse health band rollup (green/yellow/red)',
950:    handler: accountsAtRiskBandHandler,
951:  },
952:  'opportunities.pipeline_velocity': {

990:    handler: upcomingAppointmentsHandler,
991:  },

1004:  },

1019:    key: 'revenue.trend_over_range',
1020:    primaryEntity: 'revenue',

1026:    ],
